explicit_text: John Dewey was a good friend of mine for over sixty years. We shared many intellectual discussions, and his ideas greatly influenced my thinking throughout my academic career. Our friendship was a significant part of my intellectual life, especially during my time at the University of California.

explicit_text: I was called to the University of California, Berkeley to teach Latin, which was an exciting opportunity to be part of a growing university. I also created and ran the University of California Extension for over fifteen years, developing curricula that served diverse groups including immigrants, laborers, and prisoners. My work there included coordinating with the military bureau during World War I and maintaining good relations between Extension and the faculty.

explicit_text: I played a key role in transforming the Berkeley Public Library from a tiny storefront into a grand structure financed by Carnegie funds. This effort ensured the library could serve the community well and grow with the needs of Berkeley, California. It was important to me that the library be a strong cultural and educational resource for the city.

explicit_text: I created and ran the University of California Extension for over fifteen years, developing curricula that served a wide range of people including immigrants, laborers, and prisoners. My goal was to make education accessible and relevant beyond the traditional university setting.

explicit_text: I headed the relief committee during the disastrous Berkeley Fire of 1923, helping to coordinate aid and recovery efforts for the community. This was a challenging time for Berkeley, California, and I took an active role in organizing support to help residents rebuild. The experience deepened my commitment to public service in the city.
